Better Together

Combining Condition Monitoring Approaches for Maximum Insight

Home Industries Proof Resources Contact Assets

The Power of Combined Monitoring

While electrical signature analysis is powerful, combining it with other condition monitoring techniques creates comprehensive asset visibility. In this article, we explore how different approaches complement each other.

Electrical Signature Analysis + Vibration Monitoring

ESA provides early warning of electrical and mechanical faults through motor current analysis. Vibration monitoring offers direct mechanical measurement. Together, they provide redundancy and deeper fault characterization.

ESA + Temperature Monitoring

Temperature rise indicates friction, inefficiency, and bearing wear. ESA can predict these conditions electrically, while temperature confirms the mechanical effect. This combination is particularly valuable for critical systems.

ESA + Oil Analysis

For gearboxes and bearings with oil, oil analysis reveals wear debris and degradation. ESA detects the mechanical symptoms these cause. Using both together accelerates fault diagnosis.

The Recommendation

For critical assets, a multi-modal approach maximizes reliability. Start with ESA for cost-effective baseline monitoring, then add specialized sensors for high-value equipment where the combined approach delivers highest ROI.
